p1_string_of_nickels.physical_facts: Yellow pot, dense foliage, consistent circular leaf morphology.
p1_string_of_nickels.explanatory_transformations: Remained stable throughout the 5-day sequence; no significant wilting or growth spurts observed.
p1_string_of_nickels.visual_health_inference: Stable. No signs of chlorosis or turgor loss.

p2_mexican_mint.physical_facts: Black pot, central position, two primary leaves with secondary pair emerging.
p2_mexican_mint.explanatory_transformations: Gradual decline in leaf turgor observed from T-3 to current; leaves appear more pendulous.
p2_mexican_mint.visual_health_inference: Stressed. Reasoning: Leaf margins show signs of dehydration-induced drooping compared to the Earliest image.

p3_pothos.physical_facts: Black pot, two leaves, white rabbit anchor present.
p3_pothos.explanatory_transformations: Leaf necrosis at the tip of the larger leaf has progressed slightly; the leaf angle has shifted downward.
p3_pothos.visual_health_inference: Declining. Reasoning: Necrotic tissue at the leaf margin has expanded by approximately 1-2mm since the Earliest image.

p4_silver_guest.physical_facts: Black pot, shared with p2, small stature near rim.
p4_silver_guest.explanatory_transformations: Minimal change in size; however, the surrounding soil moisture appears to have fluctuated.
p4_silver_guest.visual_health_inference: Dormant/Stable. No visible signs of active growth or distress.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Soil in p2/p4 pot shows increased cracking and surface desiccation from T-3 to Current.
biome_observations.debris: White granular material (likely perlite or mineral salt deposits) has accumulated on the soil surface of p2/p4 in the most recent images.

vision_report.temporal_deltas: The sequence shows a clear transition from a moist, uniform soil state to a drier, cracked state in the black pots (p2/p3/p4) over the last 72 hours.
vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is experiencing a moderate water deficit. The Pothos (p3) is the most sensitive indicator, showing progressive necrosis, while the Mexican Mint (p2) is showing signs of wilting.
vision_report.anomalies: Accumulation of white particulate matter on the soil surface of the black pots suggests either mineral buildup from irrigation or surface-level salt crystallization.
vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical collection is currently undergoing a period of environmental stress due to soil moisture depletion. While the String of Nickels (p1) remains resilient, the Pothos and Mexican Mint are exhibiting clear visual markers of dehydration. Immediate hydration is recommended to halt the progression of leaf necrosis in p3.
